title: Quick & Easy Soda Bread
summary: This easy soda bread recipe makes a lovely crusty, fluffy loaf. It requires no prior bread-making skills, and can be whipped up in the time it takes for your oven to preheat.

method.description: Heat the oven to 230°C. Put a baking tray onto the middle rack of the oven.
Sift both flours, salt and bicarbonate of soda into a large bowl, then mix together. Make a well in the centre and pour in the buttermilk. Stir the dough together until no pockets of flour remain.
Spoon onto a large piece of baking parchment. Using wet hands, gently pat into a uniform round. Scatter the oats on top and gently pat them all over the loaf.
Transfer the dough to the preheated tray. Bake for 45-50 mins until golden brown all over. Allow to cool for at least 30 mins, until just warm, before slicing into it.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
